LDM1-CM1: Joshua Phipps was born in 1746 in Uwchlan Township, Chester County, Pennsylvania. During the Revolutionary War he served from March 1776 to March 1777 in Captain Thomas Bowyer's Company of Colonel Matthew's Virginia Regiment. He later settled in Green County, Kentucky, where he was recorded as a resident in 1820 and where he died in 1835.
LHVB-1HR: Harman Skelderman was born around 1630 and arrived in Virginia in 1673. He was a tailor by trade, and he died in Old Rappahannock, Virginia, in 1684.
LCYQ-FPS: Annis Jennet Smith was born in 1664 in Macosquin, County Londonderry, Ireland. In August 1718 she immigrated to Boston aboard the ship 'Robert,' traveling with her daughter Annis and four grandchildren. She settled in the New World and died in 1745 at Londonderry, Rockingham County, New Hampshire.
LT8S-4R2: John Garrett I was born in 1600 in Leicestershire, England, and in 1636 was noted as qualified to be called Lord. He immigrated to Virginia, in either 1631 or 1636, and later became a Quaker convert. He died in 1680 in Darby Township, Chester County, Pennsylvania.
LZR7-RMP: John Buckner Sr. was born in 1630 in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, and worked as a salter, one who sells salt. He settled in Virginia and died in 1695 at Marlfield, Gloucester County. Although it was long claimed that he brought the first printing press to America and printed the Colonial Laws in 1680, that distinction in fact belonged to Massachusetts decades earlier.
L24W-NM2: Marin Janot Lachapelle was born in France and worked as a carpenter. On 16 November 1653 he immigrated from France to New France, arriving at Montreal. He died in 1664, drowned in the Saint Lawrence River.
LCBW-D5H: Lt. James Edgar Gilliland was born in 1745 in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, and died in 1810 in Botetourt County, Virginia. A record of military service in the United States from 1812 to 1815 is also associated with his name.
LWFC-P77: Johann Sebastian Culman was baptized on 14 January 1660 in Ulmet, Kusel, Bavaria, Germany, where he also lived his life. He served as a Pfarrer, or pastor, in his community. He died in Ulmet in 1739.
LR57-V7F: Abraham Childers II was born in 1655 in Henrico, Virginia, and lived there his entire life. He was a landowner who in 1679 was assessed with two tithables, served on a jury in a 1683 horse-theft case, and over the years deeded parcels of land along the James River and Four Mile Creek to John Pleasants; by 1704 his estate held some 368 acres. He also owned an eight-year-old Indian boy named James. He died in 1693, and his will was executed in 1698.
LT7Z-D2P: Joseph Phipps II was born in 1661 in Reading, Berkshire, England. He worked as a tallow chandler and was recorded as a resident of Uwchlan Township, Chester County, Pennsylvania, in 1742. He died in 1762 at Abington, Montgomery Township, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
LZKR-VYL: Nicolas de la Plaine II was born in 1634 in Bressuire, Deux-Sevres, France, and was a French Huguenot from Poitou. By 1657 he was working as a tobacco twister in New York City, and he formally arrived there in 1664. He died in New York City in 1712.
LZ2N-T2R: Gabriel Gibaut was born in 1642 in Lusignan, Vienne, France, and was confirmed in the Catholic faith at Quebec in 1664. He came to New France as a soldier in the Sorel Company of the Carignan-Salieres Regiment, serving from 1665 through 1667. He remained in Canada and died in Montreal in 1700.
L8BQ-W6K: John Owsley II was born around 1630 and served as a Reverend. He died in 1687 in Glooston, Leicestershire, England.
LRGX-K2H: Elizabeth Underwood was born in 1632 in London, England, and made her life in colonial Virginia. Her personal life was eventful: records point to multiple marriages beginning in 1652 and a divorce in 1654 in Old Rappahannock. She wrote her will on 29 April 1672, naming a son, and after her death in 1673 it was entered for probate in Rappahannock County.
9Z1P-D6P: Rachel Clausse was born in 1617 in Sedan, Ardennes, France. She died in 1692 in Richmond, New York.
LZS9-GST: Johannes Van Gelder was born in 1635 in Haarlem, North Holland, Netherlands. In 1662 he arrived in New Netherland, settling in the colony that would become New York. He died in New York City in 1697.
LXSL-KXD: John Slaughter was born in 1595 in Upper Slaughter, Gloucestershire, England, a place tied to his family since Norman knights were granted lands and titles after the Conquest. He arrived in Virginia in 1617, held the rank of Captain, and received a land grant on Knights Creek in Essex County. He died in Essex County, Virginia, in 1655.
LB7N-H54: Benjamin Singletery was born in 1656 in Haverhill, Essex County, Massachusetts. In 1676 he served as a soldier in King Philip's War, and that summer he was paid for helping protect his town and its people. By 1695 he had moved south, settling at Red Bank near Summerville, South Carolina, where he died in 1697.
GBRB-GH8: Ann Brown was born in 1603 in England. She crossed the Atlantic and died in 1669 in Lynn, Essex County, Massachusetts.
LT8G-CZP: John Garrett II was born in 1635 in Leicestershire, England, and baptized at Claybrooke Parva in 1633. His life spanned the early colonies: he arrived in Virginia, where in 1655 he and John Hodson patented 300 acres along a branch of Bakers Creek in New Kent County, and over the following decades he is recorded in Maryland and Pennsylvania. He died in 1728 at Stafford Courthouse, Stafford County, Virginia.
LH6F-HHH: Chevalier Karl Friederich D'Arensbourg was born in 1694 in Stettin, Pomerania. A career soldier, he served on the staff of Charles XII of Sweden and rose to Captain in the Sodermanland Battalion, and at the Battle of Poltava he preserved the life of the wounded Swedish king against the Russians. In 1721 he emigrated from the island of Oesel, and by 1724 he had taken up twelve arpents along the Mississippi River on Louisiana's German Coast, where he died in 1777.
LXMC-J2G: Thomas Ewer was born in 1593 and worked as a tailor. In 1635 he immigrated aboard the ship 'James,' settling in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He died in 1638 in Charlestown.
L4QN-9TY: William Batchelor I was born in 1597 in Stanford Dingley, Berkshire, England. In 1634 he immigrated to Charlestown in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, where he lived out his days. He died in 1670, leaving an estate inventoried at 279 pounds, 11 shillings, with his will proved that June.
M12P-VT4: Abigail Stevenson was born in 1640 in Suffolk County, New York Colony, and lived her life there. She entered a second marriage at Jamaica, Queens, in 1672 and was widowed in 1696. Her parentage was the subject of debate among genealogists, with the will of Mrs. Anne Graves cited as confirming her as a daughter. She died in 1715.
LTPH-TXT: Mary Woodland was born in 1600 in England. She died in 1665 in Lynn, Essex County, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony.
LCBW-L22: Richard Greene was born in 1660 in Warwick, Kent County, Rhode Island, where he also died in 1711.
9KF7-7BB: Lt. Gen. Sir John Middleton was born in 1609 in Perth and Kinross, Scotland. A distinguished military leader, he was created 1st Earl of Middleton in 1660 and served as Governor of English Tangier. He died in 1674 at Tangier in Morocco.
GDSM-516: Albertus Hendrickson was born in 1641 in Reusel-De Mierden, North Brabant, Netherlands. On 2 May 1662 he arrived in the New World aboard the ship Golden Arent, or Golden Eagle, landing at New Amstel in present-day Delaware. He died in 1715 in Chester, Pennsylvania.
